flashcards.front: What is an array in C programming?
flashcards.back: An array is a contiguous memory data structure that stores multiple elements of the same data type under a single variable name, accessible through index positions.

flashcards.front: What is the index of the first element in a C array?
flashcards.back: The first element in a C array is always at index 0, not index 1. This is called zero-based indexing.

flashcards.front: How do you declare a one-dimensional array in C?
flashcards.back: Syntax: data_type array_name[array_size]; Example: int marks[10]; declares an array of 10 integers.

flashcards.front: What is contiguous memory allocation in arrays?
flashcards.back: Array elements are stored in adjacent memory locations consecutively. This enables fast O(1) random access to any element using its index.

flashcards.front: What is the default initialization of array elements in C?
flashcards.back: If not explicitly initialized, global and static arrays are initialized to 0. Automatic (local) arrays contain garbage values if not initialized.

flashcards.front: Can you explain array initialization at declaration time?
flashcards.back: Yes: int arr[5] = {10, 20, 30, 40, 50}; Partial initialization like int arr[5] = {1, 2}; fills remaining with 0. You can also omit size: int arr[] = {1,2,3};

flashcards.front: What is the difference between arr[5] and arr[]?
flashcards.back: arr[5] specifies exact size (5 elements). arr[] deduces size from initializer list. You cannot declare an empty array without size or initializer in C.

flashcards.front: What is array bounds checking in C?
flashcards.back: C does NOT perform automatic bounds checking. Accessing arr[-1] or arr[10] (for size 10) causes undefined behavior—program may crash or produce wrong results.

flashcards.front: How is memory allocated for an array?
flashcards.back: For int arr[5] with 4-byte ints, 20 bytes (5×4) are allocated contiguously. The array name (arr) represents the base address of the first element.

flashcards.front: What are the advantages of using arrays?
flashcards.back: 1) Store multiple values in one variable 2) Fast O(1) random access 3) Contiguous memory enables cache efficiency 4) Easy to traverse using loops.

flashcards.front: What are the limitations of arrays?
flashcards.back: 1) Fixed size (determined at compile time) 2) Cannot store different data types 3) No built-in bounds checking 4) Insertion/deletion is expensive (requires shifting).
A 2D array is an array of arrays, stored in row-major or column-major order. Syntax: int matrix[3][4]; creates 3 rows and 4 columns (12 elements).

flashcards.front: How is a 2D array stored in memory?
flashcards.back: C stores 2D arrays in row-major order—all elements of row 0 come first, then row 1, etc. The memory appears as a single contiguous block.

flashcards.front: What happens if you don't initialize a local array?
flashcards.back: Local (automatic) arrays declared inside functions are NOT automatically initialized. They contain garbage/uninitialized values and must be explicitly initialized before use.

flashcards.front: What is the relationship between array name and pointer in C?
flashcards.back: The array name (without brackets) acts as a pointer to the first element. arr == &arr[0] (same memory address). However, array name is not a modifiable l-value.
